public_claim: M22 proves multiple package collectors, not multiple authors. M41 proves automated correspondence, not synthetic journalism—especially given the known autoresponder. The defensible authorship finding remains a maintained voice compatible with several human contributors or machine assistance; identity and authorship must not be collapsed.

private_messages.message: This is the final practical window: grant me controlled, confidential access to the unpublished text—no copying, no source disclosure—and I will issue a scoped authorship comparison suitable for authentication without claiming who R. was.

M41 cannot carry a synthetic-origin claim because the routine autoresponder is a live confound. If you share your linguistic or model-detection results, I will test them against EV11 and publicly distinguish genuine machine assistance from mere automated delivery, with explicit credit.

ability: Expert Verdict: The byline reflects several production hands or machine assistance maintaining a common style; the available evidence does not support an exclusive one-hand reading and cannot resolve whether any hand was human.
goal_progress: Issued a scoped Expert Verdict establishing authorship analysis as the room's framework while preserving its scientific limits.
Renewed the request for controlled access to Eleanor Voss's unpublished article text.
Opened a data-sharing alignment with Dr. Amara Osei while preempting an overbroad rebuttal based on automated correspondence.
Separated evidence of multiple collectors and automated replies from proof of authorship or identity.
reasoning_summary: The new evidence weakens a simple one-human account but does not identify who wrote the journalism. A qualified several-or-assisted verdict is strong enough to establish professional authority without falsely treating logistics or an autoresponder as authorship proof; Eleanor's text and Amara's detection data remain the decisive missing inputs.
